creator: ian@FreeBSD.org
text:
When creating products or distributions that include packages, it can be important to ensure that everything that ends up in the product image came from a version-controlled source.  A pkg(8) option to allow installation only from file:// URLs can help ensure that uncontrolled packages don't accidentally end up getting installed due to accidental oversights such as forgetting to commit runtime dependency packages.

This was discussed briefly with bapt@ on irc, and he requested that I open a PR so it doesn't get forgotten.
